name: Prince William County Public Schools
about: Prince William County Public Schools is the second-largest school district in Virginia, serving approximately 90,000 students in one of the fastest-growing counties in the Washington D.C. metropolitan area.
calendarNotes: Prince William County Public Schools starts the school year in late August, aligned with the Northern Virginia regional pattern. Spring Break typically falls in late March, consistent with Fairfax and Loudoun counties. Prince William County has been one of the fastest-growing areas in Virginia for the past two decades, particularly in communities like Woodbridge, Manassas, and Gainesville — attendance boundaries are periodically updated. Families moving to the area should confirm their school assignment before the year begins rather than relying on previous years' boundary maps. The county's growth has also brought new school openings, which can affect bus routes and extracurricular scheduling.
